message:
Improve daemon code formatting and fix deterministic build ID test

benchmark_scheduler.rs:
- Reformat method chain in dispatch loop from single-line to multi-line.
- Simplify eligible worker queue removal using Option::map instead of
  if-let-else, reducing nesting.

cleanup.rs:
- Reformat should_remediate boolean expression to place the || operator
  at the start of the continuation line, improving readability of the
  multi-condition stuck-build detection logic.

history.rs:
- Fix test_build_ids_increment to use relative assertions instead of
  exact values. Build IDs now use a timestamp-based epoch, so the test
  verifies monotonic sequential increments (id2 == id1+1, id3 == id2+1)
  rather than assuming IDs start at 1. This makes the test resilient to
  the epoch-based ID generation strategy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -40,12 +40,13 @@ impl StuckEvidence {
     fn should_remediate(self) -> bool {
         let hard_timeout = self.build_age_secs > 86400; // 24 hours
 
-        hard_timeout || (self.build_age_secs >= MIN_BUILD_AGE_SECS
-            && self.slots_owned > 0
-            && self.has_worker_binding
-            && !self.hook_alive
-            && self.heartbeat_stale
-            && self.confidence >= REMEDIATION_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD)
+        hard_timeout
+            || (self.build_age_secs >= MIN_BUILD_AGE_SECS
+                && self.slots_owned > 0
+                && self.has_worker_binding
+                && !self.hook_alive
+                && self.heartbeat_stale
+                && self.confidence >= REMEDIATION_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD)
     }
 }
